etymology.description: semantic 扌(扌) (shǒu, "hand") + phonetic 空(空) (kōng) -> control; accuse
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 扌(扌) (shǒu, "hand"), representing action or manipulation, with 空(空) (kōng), which provides the phonetic component, to form the concept of using one's hand to exert influence or control. This manipulation or control can extend to accusations, as accusing requires taking hold of a situation verbally or legally.

examples.chinese: 她试图控制局面(她試圖控制局面)。
examples.english: She tried to control the situation.

examples.chinese: 他被指控犯了严重的罪行(他被指控犯了嚴重的罪行)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bèi zhǐkòng fànle yánzhòng de zuìxíng.
examples.english: He was accused of committing a serious crime.

examples.chinese: 人们需要学会控制自己的情绪(人們需要學會控制自己的情緒)。
examples.pinyin: Rénmen xūyào xuéhuì kòngzhì zìjǐ de qíngxù.
examples.english: People need to learn to control their emotions.
